compiled_state.title: As an engineering team lead, I want T4 kudos trigger to award points for measurable reduction in duplicate Python blocks per session
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
As an engineering team lead, I need T4 (duplicate Python block reduction) kudos trigger to award points for measurable deduplication work so that systematic refactoring is explicitly incentivized.

DONE DEFINITIONS:
- T4 integrated as function in commit-validate.sh (same integration point as Epic 1 T-series triggers)
- T4: Python files in git diff <merge_base> HEAD --name-only (deleted files excluded; .py extension only)
- Pre-session state: git show <snapshot.merge_base>:<path> for each file; if git show fails, skip that file
- Python AST subtree hashing per S1 spike findings (0ecc-9ce1): hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode()); minimum subtree size = 5 statements
- Duplicate count = number of (hash, count) pairs where count >= 2
- Kudos when post-commit count < pre-session count (reduction >= 1 absolute)
- Graceful degradation: Python unavailable → skip; merge-base unavailable → skip; file read error → skip that file
- Unit tests: no Python files, reduction detected, no reduction, Python absent, merge-base absent

CONSIDERATIONS:
- [Blocker] Depends on Epic 1 Story A (b41b-0369) for ledger infrastructure
- [Blocker] Depends on Story J for snapshot.merge_base field
- [Blocker] Depends on Spike S1 (0ecc-9ce1) for AST hashing approach
- [Scope] Deleted files excluded; extracting shared utility DOES count

comments.body: [Portability] T4 uses Python AST subtree hashing — fires only on .py files. Will silently skip on NextJS, Ruby, and Java projects. A follow-on epic tracks multi-language duplicate detection variants (ESLint/TypeScript AST, rubocop, PMD/Checkstyle). No action required in this story — document as known scope boundary.
comments.author: Claude
